message:
fix(algorithms,readwrite): use typed numeric accessors, proper format detection, and multigraph guards

Algorithms (fnx-algorithms):
- Replace .as_str().parse::<f64>() with .as_f64() across stoer_wagner,
  min_cost_flow, and numeric_assortativity_coefficient for correct
  typed attribute access instead of string round-tripping.
- Add .filter(|v| v.is_finite() && *v >= 0.0) guard on stoer_wagner
  edge weights to reject NaN/Inf/negative values.
- Fix triadic_census test to use idiomatic census.values() iterator
  instead of destructuring map entries.
- Remove stray trailing closing brace at end of file.

Python bindings (fnx-python):
- Flatten nested if-let chains in MultiDirected edge_attrs_for into
  let-chains for cleaner control flow.
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] to edge_attrs_for_directed to suppress
  unused-method warning.

Read/write (fnx-readwrite, fnx-python/readwrite):
- Replace naive string-contains directed detection
  ("edgedefault=\"directed\"" / "directed 1") with proper XML-parsed
  graphml_declares_directed() and token-parsed gml_declares_directed()
  methods that handle single-quoted attributes and ignore directed
  keywords appearing in label text.
- Add multigraph early-reject in node_link_graph to fail closed rather
  than silently dropping parallel edges.
- Add Rust unit tests for single-quote GraphML detection and GML
  false-positive directed text.

Python tests (test_io_variants):
- Add test_raw_node_link_graph_fails_closed_for_multigraph_payload.
- Add test_raw_read_graphml_detects_directed_with_single_quotes.
- Add test_raw_read_gml_ignores_directed_text_in_graph_attrs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -190,3 +190,59 @@ def test_raw_serializers_fail_closed_for_multigraphs(tmp_path: Path):
 
     with pytest.raises(TypeError):
         _fnx.write_graphml(graph, tmp_path / "graph.graphml")
+
+
+def test_raw_node_link_graph_fails_closed_for_multigraph_payload():
+    payload = {
+        "mode": "strict",
+        "directed": False,
+        "multigraph": True,
+        "graph_attrs": {},
+        "nodes": ["a", "b"],
+        "edges": [
+            {"left": "a", "right": "b", "attrs": {"w": 1}},
+            {"left": "a", "right": "b", "attrs": {"w": 2}},
+        ],
+    }
+
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
+        _fnx.node_link_graph(payload)
+
+
+def test_raw_read_graphml_detects_directed_with_single_quotes(tmp_path: Path):
+    path = tmp_path / "directed.graphml"
+    path.write_text(
+        "<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>\n"
+        "<graphml xmlns='http://graphml.graphdrawing.org/xmlns'>\n"
+        "  <graph id='G' edgedefault='directed'>\n"
+        "    <node id='a'/>\n"
+        "    <node id='b'/>\n"
+        "    <edge source='a' target='b'/>\n"
+        "  </graph>\n"
+        "</graphml>\n",
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+
+    graph = _fnx.read_graphml(path)
+
+    assert graph.is_directed()
+    assert graph.has_edge("a", "b")
+
+
+def test_raw_read_gml_ignores_directed_text_in_graph_attrs(tmp_path: Path):
+    path = tmp_path / "graph.gml"
+    path.write_text(
+        'graph [\n'
+        '  label "mentions directed 1"\n'
+        "  directed 0\n"
+        '  node [ id 0 label "a" ]\n'
+        '  node [ id 1 label "b" ]\n'
+        "  edge [ source 0 target 1 ]\n"
+        "]\n",
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+
+    graph = _fnx.read_gml(path)
+
+    assert not graph.is_directed()
+    assert graph.has_edge("a", "b")
